source_pr.title: test: make several slow package unit test support run parallel
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?
make several package unit test support run unit test parallel to speed up unit test running

### What is changed and how it works?
some unit test need to change global variables to set up running environment, thus make it impossible to run in parallel. By move these test into independent test suite and use `SerialSuites` instead of `Suite`, we can make sure they will still run in serial, so it will be safe to open the parallel flag for other tests.

Here is the result:
Before change:
```
>  go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core	110.140s

> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/session
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/session	123.002s
```

After change:
```
> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core -check.p=true
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/planner/core	41.524s

> go test -v -vet=off -p 10 -race github.com/pingcap/tidb/session -check.p=true
ok  	github.com/pingcap/tidb/session	95.673s
```

### Check List
Tests

- unit tests

Code changes

Side effects

Related changes

symptoms: Tests become unstable when packages/suites run concurrently (e.g. `go test ... -p 10 -race`), due to shared state not safe for parallel execution.
root_cause_explanation: The flakiness is caused by parallel execution with shared state: running multiple suites/packages concurrently can expose data races or unsafe shared state across tests. The PR stabilizes CI by marking specific suites as serial-only (`SerialSuites`) and moving a problematic test into a serial suite, so the overall package test execution can be parallelized safely without concurrent access to shared state.
fix_pattern: Avoid running shared-state tests in parallel: mark suites/tests as serial-only (e.g. `SerialSuites`) or isolate shared global state so package-level parallelism doesn't trigger races.
